rationale: A root-cause analysis aid; produces understanding, not a standing artifact.
Generates measured evidence from real use of variants of the output.
Defines the conditions an item must meet, populating the quality bar and detailing selected items.
Sets accessibility conditions that become part of the quality/integration standard.
A discovery/analysis aid that surfaces risks; produces no specific standing artifact.
Justifies and qualifies an initiative for inclusion/ordering in the portfolio backlog.
The canonical tool for articulating the organisation's business model.
Field research that builds the understanding of who is served and their needs.
Quantifies economic impact to inform ordering of backlog items.
Visualises flow stages over time as a delivery health indicator.
Direct conversations that articulate user needs, motivations, and pains.
A synchronisation event; produces coordination, not a standing artifact. An opportunity to inspect and adapt the team / sprint backlog. May produce a refined sprint backlog
The DoD is the team quality bar, and at scale the shared integration standard.
Produces a high-level adaptive schedule of delivery for the initiative.
Collaborative sketching that shapes the solution design.
Iterative sketching sessions that produce candidate solution designs.
Articulates what a user sees, thinks, feels and does, populating the beneficiary profile.
An epic is a large backlog item at the initiative level. Part of a backlog
Relative sizing feeds forecasting and release / delivery planning.
Define and run tests producing evidence on assumptions about feasibility, desirability and viability. Experiment is an item on a (product) backlog
A lightweight experiment producing demand evidence before building.
Breaks features into independently valuable slices that populate the backlog.
A delivery/engineering practice; produces no standing planning artifact. A feature of a product increment
A feature-level story is a backlog item that can be pulled into a cycle. Part of a backlog
A cause-and-effect analysis aid; produces understanding, not an artifact.
Cycle time, lead time and throughput are core delivery health metrics.
Breaks systems into parts, contributing to solution structure; weak artifact fit.
Given-When-Then specs define acceptance behaviour and quality conditions. Related to a backlog item
Morale tracking is a leading indicator of delivery system health.
Expert usability review yielding feedback on the output; weak artifact fit.
A reframing/ideation prompt; produces no standing artifact.
Connects deliverables to goals via actors and impacts, framing goal and outcomes. Used to create a backlog
Groups and prioritises items, informing backlog ordering.
A running blocker list reflecting delivery health; weak artifact fit.
Evaluates viability to inform portfolio allocation and ordering.
Focuses on the job the user is trying to accomplish, populating the beneficiary profile.
Maps user steps, emotions and pain points, enriching the beneficiary profile.
A one-page business model format for startups and product-market fit.
Lightweight tools that establish decision rights and alignment checks.
A general brainstorming aid; no specific artifact produced.
Categorises requirements to order the backlog and manage scope.
A goal-setting framework producing time-bound objectives with measurable results.
Captures customer problems, metrics and candidate solutions in discovery.
Links desired outcomes to opportunities and experiments in continuous discovery.
Maps outcomes to opportunities and experiments to discover value.
Research-based user profiles that directly populate the beneficiary profile.
Analyses external macro factors that inform strategic choices.
Consensus estimation that supports iteration planning.
Produces the high-level timeline sequencing initiatives against strategic themes.
Frames the core problem to solve; loosely shapes initiative purpose.
The ordered list of everything needed to add value is the initiative backlog.
Captures vision, target group, needs and goals for the product.
Decides which capabilities are delivered when across releases.
Reflection sessions that drive process improvements and updated norms.
Scores initiatives by reach, impact, confidence and effort to order the backlog.
A scoring model that prioritises and sequences backlog items.
Evaluates release scope/timing risks feeding the schedule; weak artifact fit.
Progressive elaboration that shapes the near-term and outline delivery schedule.
Maps frontstage and backstage processes that define the solution.
Visual map of service process, systems and people defining the solution.
Time-boxed exploration to reduce uncertainty; produces learning, not an artifact.
A timebox that pulls in selected items and produces an integrated output.
Visualises the journey and slices functionality, building and ordering the backlog.
Breaks large stories into smaller backlog items sized for a cycle.
Sketch sequences of user interaction that inform solution design; weak fit.
Surfaces internal/external factors that inform strategic choices.
Visual tracking of task status reflecting the cycle plan; weak artifact fit.
Directly produces the team's behavioural and practice norms.
Visualises system health to inform delivery health discussions.
Evaluates real users completing tasks, producing evidence on the output.
Structured interaction descriptions that detail scope items to build.
Analyses real journey data to find drop-offs and optimisation evidence.
Captures functionality as backlog items pulled into a team cycle.
Maps customer jobs, pains and gains to the value proposition.
Uses historical velocity as a metric and to forecast cycle capacity.
Directly produces team ground rules for collaboration and decision-making.
Sequences items by cost of delay over duration to order the backlog.
